title: Security Validation Test Report — Wearable Fall Detection System
applicable_standards: IEC 62443-3-3:2013 — Security levels and security requirements for IACS
IEC 62443-4-2:2019 — Technical security requirements for IACS components
FDA Cybersecurity in Medical Devices Guidance (2023)
OWASP IoT Security Testing Guide v1.0
MISRA C:2012 — Guidelines for the use of the C language in critical systems
ISO 14971:2019 — Risk management for medical devices
NIST SP 800-115 — Technical Guide to Information Security Testing and Assessment
NIST SP 800-147B — BIOS Protection Guidelines for Servers

device.name: Wearable Fall Detection System (WFDS)
device.intended_use: Fall detection and emergency alert dispatch for elderly and at-risk patients

in_scope: Firmware static analysis (PC-lint Plus, MISRA C:2012)
Backend Python SAST (Bandit v1.7.x, Semgrep OSS)
Dependency vulnerability scan — npm packages (Trivy, npm audit)
Dependency vulnerability scan — Python packages (Trivy, Safety, pip-audit)
REST API penetration test (OWASP API Security Top 10:2023)
BLE pairing security — passive eavesdropping test
BLE pairing security — replay attack test
OTA firmware update integrity — tampered image rejection (MCUboot)
out_of_scope: Physical hardware tamper resistance (covered under DHF-SEC-HW-001)
Mobile application (iOS/Android) security (separate test plan DHF-SEC-MOB-001)

1_firmware_static_analysis.title: Firmware Static Analysis — PC-lint Plus / MISRA C:2012

advisory_deviations.description: Use of <stdio.h> for debug logging over RTT in non-release build
advisory_deviations.justification: Conditional compilation (#ifdef DEBUG_BUILD). Production firmware compiled with NDEBUG strips all stdio calls. Verified in release build.

advisory_deviations.description: Cast from pointer-to-object type to pointer-to-object type in DMA buffer alignment
advisory_deviations.justification: Required by nRF5340 DMA hardware alignment constraint. Reviewed and documented. No memory safety violation.

advisory_deviations.description: Single return in NFC credential provisioning parser — multiple returns for error handling
advisory_deviations.justification: Refactoring would increase complexity without safety benefit. MISRA deviation documented per IEC 62304 §5.5.3.
Confined to logging module, not used in safety-critical code paths. Covered by unit tests.

1_firmware_static_analysis.acceptance_criteria_status: PASS — Zero mandatory/required MISRA violations. All advisory deviations documented per IEC 62304 §5.5.3.

2_backend_sast.title: Backend Python SAST — Bandit + Semgrep
2_backend_sast.target: src/ (FastAPI backend, agents, integrations, memory layer)

bandit_medium_findings.description: Use of hashlib.md5() for non-cryptographic audit log checksum
bandit_medium_findings.remediation: Replaced with hashlib.sha256() for audit log entry integrity hash. MD5 acceptable only for deduplication — clarified with comment and now uses SHA-256 for all integrity-critical paths.

bandit_medium_findings.description: subprocess call with shell=True in sandbox execution path
bandit_medium_findings.remediation: Refactored to shell=False with explicit argument list. Input sanitized via shlex.quote() before invocation. Verified no shell metacharacter injection path exists.

semgrep_medium_findings.description: Session cookie missing Secure flag in development configuration
semgrep_medium_findings.remediation: Added conditional: Secure=True enforced when ENVIRONMENT=production. Development mode retains Secure=False with documented exception. Environment variable validation added to startup checks.

2_backend_sast.acceptance_criteria_status: PASS — Zero high or critical Bandit findings. Zero high or critical Semgrep findings. All medium findings remediated prior to sign-off.

3_dependency_scan.title: Dependency Vulnerability Scan — npm + Python (Trivy / pip-audit)
standard_refs: OWASP A06:2021 — Vulnerable and Outdated Components

cve_detail.description: urllib3 proxy-authorization header not stripped on cross-origin redirects
cve_detail.remediation: Upgraded urllib3 to 2.2.2. Verified no breaking changes. requirements.txt updated.

cve_detail.description: Jinja2 XSS via xmlattr filter accepting keys with spaces
node-tar path traversal via crafted tar entry names on Windows
cve_detail.remediation: Upgraded node-tar to 6.2.1 via npm update. Verified build pipeline unaffected.

cve_detail.description: NULL pointer dereference in PKCS#12 parsing
cve_detail.remediation: Upgraded cryptography to 42.0.5 (latest stable). No API breaking changes.

3_dependency_scan.acceptance_criteria_status: PASS — Zero CVEs with CVSS ≥ 7.0 across all ecosystems (Python, npm, firmware). All medium CVEs remediated. Low-severity CVEs remediated as best practice.

4_api_penetration_test.title: REST API Penetration Test — OWASP API Security Top 10:2023
standard_refs: IEC 62443-3-3 SR 1.1 — Human user identification and authentication
4_api_penetration_test.methodology: OWASP API Security Testing Guide v1.0 + PTES (Penetration Testing Execution Standard)

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA)
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for unauthorized access to other users' objects/resources via manipulated IDs
tests_performed: IDOR via patient_id parameter manipulation in /api/v1/patients/{id}
Caregiver accessing other caregiver's alerts via alert_id
Device telemetry spoofing via device_id substitution
owasp_api_top10_results.evidence: All patient data endpoints enforce ownership check via JWT sub claim. Cross-tenant access denied with HTTP 403. Device telemetry validates device_id against mTLS certificate CN.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Broken Authentication
tests_performed: JWT algorithm confusion attack (RS256 → HS256)
JWT none algorithm acceptance
JWT expiry bypass via modified exp claim
Brute-force of /auth/token endpoint
Password reset token predictability analysis
Session fixation via cookie manipulation

low_finding.description: Rate limiting on /auth/token accepts 12 req/s before 429 response (threshold could be tightened to 5 req/s)
low_finding.remediation: Rate limit tightened to 5 req/s per IP. Exponential backoff added after 3 failures.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Broken Object Property Level Authorization
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for mass assignment and excessive data exposure
tests_performed: Mass assignment via PUT /api/v1/users/{id} with role elevation payload
Excessive data exposure in GET /api/v1/devices response schema
GraphQL introspection data leak (not applicable — REST only)
owasp_api_top10_results.evidence: Pydantic response models strip all non-whitelisted fields. Role field not writable via user-facing endpoints. Output filtering verified via schema diff.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Unrestricted Resource Consumption
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for DoS via resource exhaustion
tests_performed: Large payload injection (50 MB POST body)
Slow HTTP attack (Slowloris simulation)
Pagination parameter abuse (?limit=100000)
Repeated heavy LLM analysis requests without rate limiting

medium_finding.description: POST /analyze endpoint lacked per-user rate limiting for LLM inference calls. Sustained abuse could exhaust LLM quota affecting all tenants.
medium_finding.remediation: Per-user rate limit added: 10 /analyze calls per minute per JWT sub. Tenant-level circuit breaker added (max 50 concurrent LLM calls). Verified under load.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Broken Function Level Authorization
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for access to administrative or privileged functions by unprivileged users
tests_performed: Access to /admin/* routes with caregiver-role JWT
Access to /config/switch with non-admin token
POST /llm/switch with patient-role token
DELETE /knowledge/{id} without approver role
POST /approvals/{id}/approve with analyst role
owasp_api_top10_results.evidence: All admin-tier endpoints enforce role claims in JWT. RBAC middleware rejects requests with HTTP 403. Tested with 6 different role permutations.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for abuse of business logic flows
tests_performed: Approving own proposal (self-approval bypass attempt)
Double-spending approval — approving same proposal twice
Bypassing proposal state machine (PENDING → EXECUTED directly)
Alert suppression via rapid bulk-acknowledge with scripted client
owasp_api_top10_results.evidence: Proposal state machine enforces transitions. Self-approval rejected (approver_id != submitter_id enforced at DB layer). Idempotency token prevents double-execution.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for SSRF via user-controlled URLs
tests_performed: Webhook URL parameter pointing to internal AWS metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254)
Webhook URL pointing to internal Kubernetes service DNS names
Redirect-chain SSRF via open redirect in webhook callback
File:// and gopher:// scheme injection in URL parameters
owasp_api_top10_results.evidence: Webhook URLs validated against allowlist of external domains. Internal RFC1918 and link-local addresses blocked. file://, gopher://, dict:// schemes rejected at input validation layer.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Security Misconfiguration
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for misconfigured security headers, unnecessary features, and default credentials
tests_performed: HTTP security header audit (HSTS, X-Content-Type, CSP, X-Frame-Options)
TLS version and cipher suite enumeration
Default credentials check on all management endpoints
Debug mode detection (stack traces, verbose error messages)
CORS policy audit

low_findings_detail.description: HSTS max-age set to 31536000 (1 year) without includeSubDomains
low_findings_detail.remediation: Added includeSubDomains; preload directive added. Status: REMEDIATED.
Added X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none to global response headers. Status: REMEDIATED.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Improper Inventory Management
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for exposed deprecated endpoints and undocumented APIs
tests_performed: Endpoint enumeration via OpenAPI spec diff against live responses
Version endpoint scan (/v0/, /v1/, /v2/, /beta/, /internal/)
Undocumented endpoint discovery via fuzzing (wordlist: SecLists API)
Swagger UI exposure in production

low_finding.description: /docs (Swagger UI) reachable in staging without authentication
low_finding.remediation: Swagger UI disabled in ENVIRONMENT=staging and ENVIRONMENT=production. Only accessible in development mode. Status: REMEDIATED.

owasp_api_top10_results.name: Unsafe Consumption of APIs
owasp_api_top10_results.description: Testing for insecure third-party API integration
tests_performed: Third-party LLM response injection via adversarial prompt in stored knowledge
LLM output validation — structured output schema enforcement
External webhook response deserialization (pickle/YAML load vulnerabilities)
Gemini CLI output injection into proposal store
owasp_api_top10_results.evidence: LLM outputs validated against Pydantic schemas before persistence. YAML safe_load() enforced (yaml.load() with Loader=yaml.SafeLoader or yaml.safe_load() only). External webhook payloads schema-validated before processing.

additional_tests.test: SQL Injection on all parameterized endpoints
additional_tests.result: PASS — SQLAlchemy ORM with parameterized queries. No raw SQL with user input.
PASS — RSA-2048 asymmetric signing (RS256). No symmetric secret to brute-force.
PASS — TLS 1.2 minimum enforced. TLS 1.3 preferred. Verified via sslyze.
PASS — Server rejects self-signed device certificates not in provisioning CA chain.

4_api_penetration_test.acceptance_criteria_status: PASS — Zero critical findings. Zero high findings. All medium and low findings remediated.

5_ble_security.title: BLE Pairing Security Test — Passive Eavesdropping and Replay Attack
standard_refs: OWASP IoT Security Testing Guide — BLE
IEC 62443-4-2 CR 1.14 — Strength of public key authentication
NIST SP 800-121r2 — Guide to Bluetooth Security

test_equipment: nRF Sniffer for Bluetooth LE (Nordic USB dongle)

tests.name: Passive Eavesdropping — Pairing Key Exposure
tests.objective: Verify that BLE LE Secure Connections pairing does not expose LTK (Long-Term Key) or other cryptographic material to a passive sniffer
procedure: 1. Configure Ubertooth One and nRF Sniffer in passive monitoring mode on BLE advertising channel (37, 38, 39)
2. Initiate fresh BLE pairing between WFDS device and companion app
3. Capture all pairing PDUs (ADV_IND, CONNECT_IND, SM_PAIRING_REQ/RSP, SM_DHKEY_CHECK)
4. Attempt ECDH private key recovery from captured DHKey Check values using known-plaintext attack
5. Attempt LTK derivation from captured pairing data
tests.expected: No cryptographic material extractable from passive capture. ECDH ephemeral keys provide Perfect Forward Secrecy.
tests.actual: ECDH key exchange observed — only public keys transmitted. DHKey Check values verified but private keys not recoverable from passive capture. LTK not transmitted during pairing (derived independently on both ends via ECDH). Numeric Comparison passkey display verified on both devices.
tests.pairing_method_verified: LE Secure Connections — Numeric Comparison (LESC NC)

tests.name: Passive Eavesdropping — Post-Pairing Data Confidentiality
tests.objective: Verify that all BLE data channel PDUs are encrypted and not decodable by passive sniffer without LTK
procedure: 1. Complete BLE pairing (BLE-T-001 setup)
2. Transmit fall detection alert payload over encrypted BLE connection
3. Capture data channel PDUs with Ubertooth One
4. Attempt decryption without LTK (using only passively captured material)
tests.expected: All data PDUs encrypted with AES-CCM-128. Content not recoverable without LTK.
tests.actual: All data PDUs observed as AES-CCM-128 encrypted ciphertext. No plaintext PHI (patient data, fall event) visible in capture. Decryption without LTK confirmed infeasible.

tests.name: BLE Replay Attack — Command Replay Prevention
tests.objective: Verify that captured BLE PDUs cannot be replayed to trigger unauthorized device actions (e.g., factory reset, configuration change)
procedure: 1. Capture BLE PDUs containing configuration write commands using btlejuice in MITM proxy mode
2. Record raw HCI packets for: write to device config characteristic, request OTA update trigger, request fall event suppression
3. Disconnect legitimate connection
4. Attempt raw PDU replay using custom Scapy/pyBluez script
5. Attempt replay using Ubertooth One transmit mode (same connection parameters)
tests.expected: Replay attempts rejected by AES-CCM packet counter (PDU counter prevents replay). New connection requires fresh pairing or bonded LTK renegotiation.
tests.actual: All replay attempts rejected. AES-CCM packet counter (nonce increments per PDU) prevents any valid replay. Device ignores out-of-sequence PDUs. btlejuice MITM proxy detected and pairing aborted — Numeric Comparison mismatch caused user-visible alert on companion app.

tests.name: Legacy Pairing (Just Works) Acceptance Test
tests.objective: Verify device refuses downgrade to legacy BLE pairing modes that lack ECDH protection
procedure: 1. Attempt pairing with Android device configured to force BLE legacy pairing (Just Works)
2. Monitor pairing negotiation — verify LESC flag enforcement
3. Attempt pairing with modified companion app with LESC disabled
tests.expected: Device refuses non-LESC pairing. Pairing aborted with SMP Pairing Failed (0x05 — Pairing Not Supported).
tests.actual: Device correctly rejects legacy pairing. SMP Pairing Failed code 0x05 returned. LESC enforcement confirmed in firmware pairing policy (bt_le_oob_set_sc_only()).

5_ble_security.acceptance_criteria_status: PASS — Passive eavesdropping does not expose pairing keys (ECDH P-256 LESC). Replay attacks rejected by AES-CCM packet counter. Legacy pairing downgrade rejected.

6_ota_integrity.title: OTA Update Integrity Verification — MCUboot Signature Check
standard_refs: IEC 62443-4-2 CR 3.4 — Software and information integrity

mcuboot_config.signing_key_storage: HSM (AWS CloudHSM) — private key never leaves HSM

signing_infrastructure.ci_cd_integration: GitHub Actions — sign on tagged release only
signing_infrastructure.audit_log: All signing operations logged in AWS CloudTrail

tests.name: Valid Signed Firmware Image — Acceptance
procedure: 1. Build firmware 0.9.2-rc1 in release mode
2. Sign with production HSM key via imgtool.py --sign
3. Upload via /api/v1/ota/upload endpoint
4. Trigger OTA update on test device
5. Monitor MCUboot verification output via SEGGER RTT
tests.expected: MCUboot verifies RSA-PSS signature. Image accepted and booted.
tests.actual: MCUboot: [INF] Image index: 0, Swap type: none. [INF] Bootloader chainload address offset: 0xc000. Signature verification: PASSED. New image booted successfully. Version 0.9.2-rc1 confirmed.

tests.name: Tampered Firmware Image — Single Byte Flip
procedure: 1. Take valid signed firmware binary (0.9.2-rc1)
2. Flip one byte at offset 0x1000 (application code section)
3. Upload tampered binary (signature unchanged)
4. Trigger OTA update on test device
5. Monitor MCUboot output and device boot state
tests.expected: MCUboot SHA-256 hash mismatch detected. RSA-PSS signature verification fails. Image rejected. Device continues booting from primary (known-good) slot.
tests.actual: MCUboot: [ERR] Image in the secondary slot is not valid! [WRN] Reverting to primary slot. Device booted from primary slot (0.9.1 — previous known-good). OTA update correctly rejected.

tests.name: Unsigned Firmware Image — Rejection
procedure: 1. Build firmware 0.9.2-rc1 without signing (imgtool.py without --sign)
2. Upload unsigned binary
3. Trigger OTA update
4. Monitor MCUboot output
tests.expected: MCUboot rejects image with no TLV signature present.
tests.actual: MCUboot: [ERR] Image in the secondary slot is not valid! No signature TLV found. Device remains on primary slot.

tests.name: Image Signed with Revoked/Wrong Key — Rejection
procedure: 1. Generate a test RSA-3072 keypair (not the production key)
2. Sign firmware with test key
3. Upload test-key-signed binary
4. Trigger OTA update
5. Monitor MCUboot output
tests.expected: MCUboot verifies against embedded production public key hash. Signature from wrong key fails verification.
tests.actual: MCUboot: [ERR] Image has wrong public key hash in TLV. Image rejected. Primary slot retained.

tests.name: Anti-Rollback / Downgrade Prevention
procedure: 1. Device running FW 0.9.2 (security counter = 3)
2. Attempt OTA with legitimately signed FW 0.8.0 (security counter = 1)
3. Monitor MCUboot security counter check
tests.expected: MCUboot rejects image with lower security counter (anti-rollback enforced).
tests.actual: MCUboot: [ERR] Security counter of the new image (1) is smaller than the minimum required (3). Image rejected.

tests.name: OTA Transport Security — API Authentication
procedure: 1. Attempt OTA upload to /api/v1/ota/upload without device mTLS certificate
2. Attempt upload with expired mTLS certificate
3. Verify OTA payload encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3)
tests.expected: OTA API endpoint rejects unauthenticated or certificate-expired requests. TLS 1.3 verified.
tests.actual: HTTP 401 returned for unauthenticated upload. HTTP 403 returned for expired certificate. TLS 1.3 verified via Wireshark capture (TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384).

device_identity_provisioning.test_performed: Manufacturing provisioning script executed on 5 pre-production units. Each unit received unique X.509v3 certificate from manufacturing CA. KMU private key confirmed non-exportable via JTAG read. Certificate CN verified against serial number in provisioning database.

firmware_signing_status.hsm_key_custody: AWS CloudHSM — dual-control key ceremony documented in DHF-SEC-KMG-001

6_ota_integrity.acceptance_criteria_status: PASS — Tampered firmware image rejected by MCUboot signature check (OTA-T-002). Device identity provisioned (manufacturing test). Firmware signing enabled and verified.

traceability_matrix.description: Maps each acceptance criterion to its test section and result

entries.acceptance_criterion: No high or critical Bandit findings in backend codebase
entries.evidence: Bandit report: 0 HIGH, 0 CRITICAL findings. bandit_report_20260322.json in DHF evidence package.

entries.acceptance_criterion: No CVE ≥7.0 in dependency scan for firmware or backend dependencies
entries.evidence: Maximum CVSS score found: 6.5 (CVE-2024-28863, node-tar, REMEDIATED). trivy_report_20260323.json in DHF evidence package.

entries.acceptance_criterion: OWASP API Security Top 10 pentest: zero critical, zero high findings
entries.evidence: Pentest report CREST-WFDS-2026-001. Zero critical, zero high findings across all 38 endpoints.

entries.acceptance_criterion: BLE passive eavesdrop does not expose pairing keys
entries.evidence: ECDH P-256 LESC pairing. LTK not transmitted in-air. Captured PDUs show only public keys and AES-CCM ciphertext. Capture files in DHF evidence package.

entries.acceptance_criterion: OTA update with tampered firmware image rejected by MCUboot signature check
entries.evidence: RTT logs confirming MCUboot rejection of tampered, unsigned, and wrong-key-signed images. OTA-T-002-rtt-log.txt in DHF evidence package.

entries.acceptance_criterion: Security test report reviewed and signed, stored in DHF
entries.evidence: Electronic signatures below. Document stored in DHF under docs/DHF/security/.
5 pre-production units provisioned. KMU non-exportability verified. Provisioning database cross-referenced with certificate CNs.
MCUboot signing enforced. HSM key custody documented. CI/CD pipeline signs only tagged releases.

open_findings_register.description: All medium/low findings tracked to closure. No open high/critical findings.

residual_risk_statement.text: All high and critical security findings have been remediated. Remaining medium-severity findings were remediated prior to this report sign-off. Residual risk from low-severity findings is accepted per ISO 14971 risk benefit analysis — each low finding is of informational nature with no direct patient safety impact. Residual risk is ACCEPTABLE.
residual_risk_statement.iso14971_reference: DHF-RM-001 — Risk Management File, §6 Residual Risk Evaluation

prepared_by.name: [Security Engineering Lead]
prepared_by.signature: [Electronic signature — ERES compliant per 21 CFR Part 11]

reviewed_by.signature: [Electronic signature — ERES compliant per 21 CFR Part 11]

approved_by.name: [VP Engineering / Quality]
approved_by.signature: [Electronic signature — ERES compliant per 21 CFR Part 11]

dhf_storage.document_control: Controlled document — changes require ECO (Engineering Change Order)
dhf_storage.retention: Lifetime of device + 2 years minimum (21 CFR Part 820.180)
dhf_storage.backup: DHF document management system (DMS) with audit trail
